codeReview.summary: The module and tests are substantial and mostly aligned with the feature scope, but two blocking correctness gaps remain: operator normalization can crash on malformed operator types, and null-to-empty-string coercion is not applied to all schema-required string fields as specified.

issues.description: _normalize_operators assumes operator is a string and calls `op.lower().strip()` unguarded. If extracted data contains a non-string operator (e.g., integer), post_process raises AttributeError and stops processing. Reproduced with a local snippet (`operator: 5`) yielding `AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'lower'`.

Null coercion implementation is partial: `_coerce_null_strings` handles selected fields (title, provision/definition fields, parameter unit, column name, condition parameter) but does not coerce other schema-required string fields (e.g., `source.standard`, `source.section`, `metadata.extracted_by`, `metadata.qc_status`, `data.expression` for formulas). This conflicts with the feature requirement to coerce null for fields where schema requires `type: "string"`. Reproduced with `source.standard = None`, which remains `None` after post_process.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Procedure-adherence signaling appears inconsistent: the python-worker skill specifies a real-data manual verification step for post-processing features, but this run did not execute that check while handoff feedback still marked followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md lines 52-53 require running post-processing on `output/raw/asce722-ch26.json`; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `fb685aee-6108-4742-b6b1-094867929f22` shows pytest/import/CLI checks but no command against `output/raw/asce722-ch26.json`; handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

summary: Reviewed feature post-processor-module (commit 35f19cc6c58d684582aa0a404c9f9b835cecaa2b): implementation is close but fails review due to two blocking defects in robustness/completeness of post-processing behavior.
